kits.title: Research Source Scout
kits.summary: A research agent buys ranked market, academic, and news sources, then stops when its mandate cannot fund a fourth source.
kits.businessLogic: Rank sources by relevance, purchase them sequentially, merge provenance-tagged results, and preserve the locked status of anything the contract rejects.

negativePath.outcome: The fourth purchase is rejected on-chain and an accepted proof cannot unlock another source.

kits.fixtures: Four stable JSON sources with fixed identifiers, prices, relevance scores, and expected merged provenance.

kits.title: Page Snapshot Meter
kits.summary: An agent buys a normalized page snapshot containing metadata, links, readability statistics, and a content hash.
kits.businessLogic: Sanitize fixture HTML, canonicalize its structure, extract reviewable metadata, and hash the normalized result for cache-safe delivery.

negativePath.outcome: Payment for one page cannot unlock another page and malformed fixture HTML fails before settlement.

kits.fixtures: Bundled HTML pages and independently checked normalized snapshot JSON; the kit makes no browser-rendering claim.

kits.title: Existing API Tollgate
kits.summary: Place REAPP in front of an existing read-only API without rewriting the protected backend.
kits.businessLogic: Map only allowlisted upstream routes, strip sensitive headers, enforce an upstream deadline, and attach response provenance without accepting arbitrary target URLs.

negativePath.outcome: Unknown upstream routes fail before payment and another merchant's mandate cannot unlock the gateway.

kits.fixtures: A local upstream service with stable inventory, weather, and account-status payloads and explicit route mappings.

kits.title: Paid Tool Gateway
kits.summary: Publish free typed tool discovery while charging for selected tool results through a bounded HTTP integration.
kits.businessLogic: Separate free discovery from paid execution, validate declared input schemas, enforce a client price ceiling, and return typed result provenance.

negativePath.outcome: An unknown schema or a price above the consumer ceiling is rejected without attempting payment.

kits.fixtures: Deterministic supply-risk, exchange-rate, and document-classification tools with typed inputs and outputs.

kits.title: Coding Agent Purchase Hook
kits.summary: A coding agent encounters a paid artifact, checks policy, settles once, retries once, and writes the result atomically.
kits.businessLogic: Apply a host allowlist and price policy, permit one payment retry, verify the artifact hash, and write the accepted patch without partial files.

negativePath.outcome: A repeated 402 after settlement stops immediately and a duplicate invocation does not pay twice.

kits.fixtures: Three issue identifiers with deterministic patch artifacts, expected hashes, and one intentionally invalid response.

kits.title: Discoverable Service Bazaar
kits.summary: An agent discovers services, scores declared schemas and prices, then pays the best compatible fixed merchant.
kits.businessLogic: Search a free catalog, check input-output compatibility, rank by deterministic quality signals, and treat discovery metadata as advisory rather than financial authority.

negativePath.outcome: A listing that advertises the wrong merchant cannot be unlocked even when it ranks first.

kits.fixtures: Six signed catalog listings with stable schemas, prices, quality signals, merchant addresses, and outputs.

kits.title: Agent Reputation Snapshot
kits.summary: Buy a time-bounded reputation snapshot derived from signed execution events without treating a score as payment authority.
kits.businessLogic: Verify an issuer allowlist, exclude stale events, weight completed and failed outcomes, and return evidence references beside the score.

negativePath.outcome: Events from an untrusted issuer or outside the freshness window are excluded and wrong-merchant settlement fails.

kits.fixtures: Run-relative signed events produced from a clearly identified local Ed25519 fixture issuer.

kits.title: Multi-Agent Workflow Router
kits.summary: A planner purchases research, verification, and synthesis artifacts as an explicit dependency graph.
kits.businessLogic: Evaluate DAG dependencies, allocate stage budgets, propagate source provenance, and stop the workflow when verification fails.

negativePath.outcome: Failed verification prevents synthesis and insufficient remaining budget blocks the next stage on-chain.

kits.fixtures: Three deterministic case graphs with specialist outputs and one intentional verification failure.

kits.title: Verifiable Compute Broker
kits.summary: Buy a bounded cryptographic workload and verify the returned digest independently on the consumer side.
kits.businessLogic: Apply fixed work tiers and CPU ceilings, execute a deterministic hash chain, and require the consumer to recompute the expected digest.

negativePath.outcome: A tier outside the mandate fails while unknown seeds and work above the server ceiling never execute.

kits.fixtures: Fixed seeds and work tiers with independently calculated digest vectors and runtime ceilings.

kits.title: Private Test Runner
kits.summary: A coding agent buys a hidden-test report for a known patch without receiving the private test source.
kits.businessLogic: Select an allowlisted patch, run it against server-only tests, redact sensitive output, and hash the final pass-or-fail report.

negativePath.outcome: Path traversal and unknown patch identifiers are rejected while a known bad patch returns a paid failing report.

kits.fixtures: A small sample repository, three fixed patches, and fulfillment-only tests with stable report hashes.

kits.title: Build Notary
kits.summary: Buy a signed statement binding an artifact hash to its dependency inventory and reproducible build metadata.
kits.businessLogic: Verify the requested SHA-256, inventory a fixed lockfile, canonicalize the statement, sign it, and verify that signature offline.

negativePath.outcome: A tampered artifact fails local signature or hash verification and one build's payment cannot fetch another statement.

kits.fixtures: Two small artifacts, exact dependency inventories, and a clearly identified local fixture signer.

kits.title: Model Route Bazaar
kits.summary: Compare provider quotes, choose the lowest-cost route satisfying quality and latency policy, and buy its result.
kits.businessLogic: Check quote freshness, score price-quality-latency policy, verify the provider merchant, and preserve output provenance and fallback reasoning.

negativePath.outcome: A stale quote or provider-merchant mismatch is rejected and a route above remaining budget is skipped.

kits.fixtures: Clearly labeled fixture providers with stable quotes, scores, and precomputed outputs; no live-model claim.

kits.title: Rights Receipt
kits.summary: Purchase an exact asset-license version and receive content plus a receipt binding terms, asset hash, and payment transaction.
kits.businessLogic: Address content by hash, select versioned plain-language license terms, construct a local receipt, and verify every binding before use.

negativePath.outcome: Payment for another asset or license version cannot unlock the requested content.

kits.fixtures: Bundled text, image, and data assets with stable hashes and clearly labeled sample license terms.

kits.title: Data Owner Gateway
kits.summary: A data owner operates the merchant endpoint and receives payment directly when an agent unlocks that owner's dataset.
kits.businessLogic: Resolve an owner-specific fixed merchant, verify dataset freshness and permitted-use metadata, and return content provenance without claiming revenue splits.

negativePath.outcome: A dataset mapped to another owner cannot be unlocked with the current merchant-scoped mandate.

kits.fixtures: Three owner-specific datasets with stable hashes, freshness timestamps, and use-policy metadata.

kits.title: Human Review Outbox
kits.summary: An agent buys a signed review decision only after a case reaches a ready state and the mandate remains valid.
kits.businessLogic: Track pending and ready states, enforce an SLA timestamp, validate the reviewer signature, and durably accept exactly one decision.

negativePath.outcome: Authority that expires while the case waits blocks settlement and stale or unsigned decisions fail local validation.

kits.fixtures: A clearly labeled fixture reviewer writes deterministic signed decisions and one deliberately stale decision.

kits.title: Cold-Chain Passport
kits.summary: Buy a shipment passport summarizing custody handoffs, temperature excursions, and sensor continuity.
kits.businessLogic: Calculate threshold duration, detect telemetry gaps, order custody transfers, and bind every finding to hashed evidence references.

negativePath.outcome: Expired authority blocks delivery and missing sensor intervals produce an explicit incomplete result rather than a false pass.

kits.fixtures: Deterministic telemetry and custody records for normal, breached, and incomplete shipments.

kits.title: Carbon-Aware Run Window
kits.summary: Buy the lowest-emission execution window that still meets a workload deadline and capacity requirement.
kits.businessLogic: Search bounded time windows, score carbon and capacity, enforce a deadline, and reject forecasts that are too old for scheduling.

negativePath.outcome: A stale forecast or infeasible window returns an explicit business outcome and expired authority cannot buy refreshed data.

kits.fixtures: Fixed hourly intensity, capacity, and workload records with independently checked optimal windows.

kits.title: Fleet Corridor Authority
kits.summary: A fleet agent buys successive route clearances until an operator revokes its mandate and the next segment fails closed.
kits.businessLogic: Validate segment order and weather epoch, maintain route provenance, and require a fresh contract-authorized purchase for each clearance.

negativePath.outcome: After two accepted segments the user revokes authority and the third settlement is rejected by the contract.

kits.fixtures: Three deterministic route segments with fixed weather epochs, constraints, and expected clearance decisions.

kits.title: Payment Receipt Firewall
kits.summary: A hardened merchant demonstrates persistent replay admission, exact request binding, and safe recovery after restart.
kits.businessLogic: Persist used-proof claims, compare origin, method, and resource, record exact response bytes before delivery, and recover the same claim after restart.

negativePath.outcome: Re-signing an old transaction for a new resource returns 409 and a process restart never reopens the proof.

kits.fixtures: Three stable vault resources while every payment proof remains live Stellar testnet evidence.

kits.title: Procurement Guard
kits.summary: A purchasing agent compares approved vendor quote packs and buys only a plan permitted by budget, merchant scope, and deadline.
kits.businessLogic: Apply a vendor allowlist, optimize cost and lead time, reject stale quotes, and explain why the selected purchase plan satisfies policy.

negativePath.outcome: An unauthorized vendor remains blocked when cheaper and the final otherwise-valid pack is rejected after budget exhaustion.

kits.fixtures: Stable vendor catalogs, delivery windows, request constraints, and independently checked optimal plans.
